project: Hal Cloud Daytona Sandbox Orchestration v1
description: Terminal-first cloud orchestration for running hal workloads in Daytona sandboxes with durable lifecycle state, lease-safe concurrency, adapter parity across Postgres and Turso, auth profile management, idempotent GitHub side effects, and strict secret redaction.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create runs table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want durable run records so that orchestration can recover after restarts.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create attempts table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want attempt-level state so that heartbeat and retry behavior is explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: Enforce at most one active attempt per `run_id`.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create events table
userStories.description: As an operator, I want durable run timelines so that status and logs read authoritative history.
acceptanceCriteria: Events are append-only in normal flow.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create idempotency table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want persisted idempotency keys so that retries cannot duplicate external side effects.
acceptanceCriteria: Duplicate insert path returns deterministic `duplicate_key` domain error.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create auth profiles table
userStories.description: As an operator, I want reusable auth profiles so that credentials are linked once and reused by workers.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create auth profile locks table
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want lease-based locks so that shared subscription sessions are concurrency-safe.
acceptanceCriteria: Add constraint preventing duplicate active lock rows for the same `(auth_profile_id, run_id)`.

userStories.title: [Backend] Define Store interface and domain errors
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a single persistence contract so that orchestration logic is adapter-agnostic.
acceptanceCriteria: Define Store methods for enqueue, claim, transition, attempt heartbeats, events, idempotency, auth profile read/update, and lock acquire/renew/release.
Service layer compiles without adapter-specific imports.

userStories.title: [Backend] Add adapter contract test suite skeleton
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want adapter-neutral tests so that correctness rules are defined once.
acceptanceCriteria: Add contract test cases for enqueue, atomic claim, one-active-attempt invariant, heartbeat renew, and transition guards.
Add parallel claim test that asserts one winner per run.
Test suite accepts adapter factory injection via `newStoreForTest`.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Postgres enqueue/claim operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Postgres run-claim primitives so that workers can safely pick work.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Postgres methods for run enqueue and atomic claim.
Claim SQL is concurrency-safe under parallel workers with one winner per run.
Add focused tests for enqueue and one-winner claim behavior.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Postgres auth lock lease operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Postgres lock primitives so that auth profile concurrency is enforceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Postgres methods for auth lock acquire, renew, and release.
Enforce lease expiry semantics on renew operations.
Add focused tests for lock acquisition conflict and stale-lock reclaim paths.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Turso enqueue/claim operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Turso run-claim primitives so that workers can safely pick work.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Turso methods for run enqueue and atomic claim.
Claim behavior matches one-winner semantics under contention.
Add focused tests for enqueue and one-winner claim behavior.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Turso auth lock lease operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Turso lock primitives so that auth profile concurrency is enforceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Turso methods for auth lock acquire, renew, and release.
Enforce lease expiry semantics on renew operations.
Add focused tests for lock acquisition conflict and stale-lock reclaim paths.

userStories.title: [Backend] Run parity benchmark harness for both adapters
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I want comparable adapter metrics so that default DB choice is evidence-based.
acceptanceCriteria: Harness executes scenarios `claim_contention`, `heartbeat_renewal`, and `auth_lock_contention` for both adapters.
Harness output schema uses fixed JSON keys: `scenario`, `adapter`, `runs`, `errors`, `claim_p95_ms`, `heartbeat_p95_ms`, `duplicate_claims`, `lock_overcommit_violations`.
Generate a report artifact for each adapter run.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement submit validation and enqueue
userStories.description: As an operator, I want invalid runs rejected before queueing so that only executable runs enter the system.
acceptanceCriteria: Reject profiles not in `linked` status and reject engine/provider mismatch.
Enforce provider policy allow/deny before creating queued run.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement atomic claim and lock acquisition transaction
userStories.description: As a worker, I want ownership acquisition to be all-or-nothing so that claimed work is always lock-protected.
acceptanceCriteria: One transaction transitions one eligible run to `claimed` and creates an attempt row.
The same transaction acquires auth lock lease.
Lock acquisition failure rolls back run claim and attempt creation.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement heartbeat renewals
userStories.description: As a worker, I want lease renewals so that healthy attempts are not reclaimed.
acceptanceCriteria: Heartbeat updates lock `heartbeat_at` and extends lock `lease_expires_at`.
Lease loss emits terminal event `lease_lost` and stops worker execution.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement stale-attempt reconciler
userStories.description: As an operator, I want stale attempts detected and closed automatically so that stuck runs are recoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: Reconciler scans active attempts where `lease_expires_at < now`.
Reconciler marks stale attempts terminal with `error_code=stale_attempt`.
Reconciler releases stale auth lock leases.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement retry scheduler with backoff
userStories.description: As an operator, I want deterministic retry transitions so that retryable failures are handled predictably.
acceptanceCriteria: Retry policy supports configurable `max_attempts` and exponential backoff.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement sandbox provisioning
userStories.description: As a worker, I want deterministic sandbox creation so that each attempt has isolated runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Provision a Daytona sandbox from configured template/image.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement sandbox teardown workflow
userStories.description: As a worker, I want teardown to always run on terminal paths so that resources are not leaked.
acceptanceCriteria: Execute teardown on success and failure terminal paths.
Emit `teardown_done` event after teardown call completes.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement bootstrap setup steps
userStories.description: As a worker, I want deterministic environment setup so that Hal execution starts from a known baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: Run tool checks for required binaries before clone.
Clone/fetch repository and checkout target branch.
Run `hal init` only when `.hal/` directory is missing.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Hal execution runner
userStories.description: As a worker, I want a controlled execution step so that run mode is explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: Support execution mode config values `until_complete` and `bounded_batch`.
Persist execution start and finish events with the mode used.
Capture command exit status for classifier input.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement failure classifier
userStories.description: As an operator, I want consistent failure codes so that retry decisions are deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: Map provider, preflight, bootstrap, and runtime failures to classifier codes.
Retry eligibility uses classifier code mapping only.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement hard-timeout enforcement
userStories.description: As an operator, I want overdue runs terminated automatically so that system resources are bounded.
acceptanceCriteria: Submit sets `deadline_at` from default or policy.
Reconciler marks runs beyond deadline as `failed` with `error_code=run_timeout`.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement auth materialization with least-privilege perms
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want worker credential materialization to be secure by default so that secret exposure risk is minimized.
acceptanceCriteria: Materialize auth artifacts from `secret_ref` only at runtime.
Create auth directories with mode `0700` and files with mode `0600`.
Remove materialized files in teardown path.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement provider preflight and compatibility checks
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want headless validation before Hal execution so that workers fail fast on incompatible environments.
acceptanceCriteria: Execute provider-specific non-interactive preflight command before run step.
Validate runtime metadata compatibility for OS, architecture, and CLI major/minor.
Fail with explicit code `auth_profile_incompatible` when metadata checks fail.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement credential writeback with version checks
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want refreshed credentials persisted safely so that concurrent updates do not corrupt profile state.
acceptanceCriteria: Detect changed auth artifacts and write back encrypted secret via Auth Broker.
Update uses optimistic version check on `auth_profiles.version`.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement deterministic redaction rule engine
userStories.description: As an operator, I want deterministic masking so that secret handling is testable.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement explicit rules for bearer tokens, GitHub PATs, device codes, session cookies, and API keys.
Each rule has fixture-based unit tests with expected masked output.

userStories.title: [Backend] Integrate redaction into persistence and streaming pipelines
userStories.description: As an operator, I want every log path redacted before visibility so that no secret reaches storage or CLI output.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply redaction before writing bootstrap, preflight, runtime, and error events.
Apply the same redaction before streaming logs to CLI.
Integration tests verify no raw fixture secret reaches DB rows or CLI stream output.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement idempotent PR create side effect
userStories.description: As an operator, I want retries to reuse PR creation results so that duplicate PRs are never created.
acceptanceCriteria: PR creation uses a deterministic idempotency key.
Existing key returns stored PR reference without external create call.
Retry test verifies exactly one external PR create call.

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement idempotent PR update/comment side effects
userStories.description: As an operator, I want retries to avoid duplicate PR follow-up actions so that external side effects remain idempotent.
acceptanceCriteria: PR update and comment actions use deterministic idempotency keys.
Existing key returns stored result without duplicate external call.
Retry test verifies no duplicate comment or update side effects.

userStories.title: [Backend] Enforce cancellation propagation and claim exclusion
userStories.description: As an operator, I want cancellation intent enforced by workers and scheduler logic so that canceled runs stop promptly and do not re-enter execution.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker loop checks cancel intent each heartbeat interval and stops active execution when set.
Claim query excludes runs in terminal `canceled` state.
Retry scheduler skips runs marked canceled.

userStories.title: [Backend] Enforce revoked-profile renewal denial
userStories.description: As an operator, I want revoked profiles unusable by active workers so that compromised credentials cannot continue to execute.
acceptanceCriteria: Lock renew operation fails with `profile_revoked` when profile state is `revoked`.
Worker receiving `profile_revoked` stops execution and releases lock.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud submit` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a clear submit command response so that both automation and humans can launch runs reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: `--json` returns machine-readable error codes on validation failures.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud status` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want quick visibility into run progress and health so that I can decide whether to wait, retry, or cancel.
acceptanceCriteria: `--json` output key set is stable and documented.
Unknown run ID returns non-zero exit and `not_found` error code.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud logs` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want historical and live run logs in terminal so that I can debug execution without a dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: `logs <run-id>` returns events ordered by ascending timestamp.
Output never contains unredacted fixture secret tokens.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud cancel` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to request cancellation from terminal so that I can stop unwanted runs quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: `cancel <run-id>` sets cancel intent and returns current cancel state.
Command supports `--json` output with stable fields `run_id`, `cancel_requested`, `status`.
Unknown run ID returns non-zero exit with `not_found` error code.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud auth link` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want interactive profile linking outside worker sandboxes so that worker runs remain headless.
acceptanceCriteria: `auth link --provider --profile` initiates provider flow from operator environment.
Command stores encrypted secret reference on success.
Profile status transitions to `linked` only after validation passes.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud auth import` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to import local authenticated artifacts so that I can bootstrap profiles when interactive link is unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: Imported artifacts are encrypted and stored as a secret reference.
Import path records an audit event with profile ID and provider.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud auth status` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want profile readiness visibility so that I know whether a profile can be used for submit.
acceptanceCriteria: `auth status <profile>` shows provider, profile state, lock owner, lock expiry, and compatibility summary.
Missing profile returns non-zero exit with `not_found` error code.
`--json` output key set is stable and documented.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud auth validate` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want explicit profile validation before submit so that incompatible credentials fail before queueing runs.

userStories.title: [CLI] Add `hal-cloud auth revoke` command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want fast profile revocation so that compromised credentials can be disabled immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Command emits audit event including profile ID and provider.
Command returns non-zero exit with `not_found` when profile is missing.
